title: Understanding Common Error Types - Run-time errors

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we will explore run-time errors, which are errors that occur while your program is running.
- Run-time errors in C++ occur during program execution, causing unexpected behavior or crashes due to issues like invalid input or memory problems.
- Identifying and resolving run-time errors can be challenging since the program appears to run fine until it encounters the error. 

---

**Identifying Run-time Errors in C++**
1. **Arithmetic Operations**
```cpp
int result = 10 / 0;  // Division by zero – causes a crash at run-time
// Expected error:
// inf (infinity)
```
2. **Infinite Loops**
```cpp
while (true) {
    // This loop will never end – consumes CPU and freezes the program
}
```
3. **Invalid Input**
```cpp
int age;
cin >> age;  // If user enters text instead of a number, the program fails
```
